description: Canonical parameter dictionary for precision voltage references, covering both series (3-terminal) and shunt (2-terminal) topologies with one vocabulary. The reference voltage is output_voltage for a series part and the reverse breakdown voltage for a shunt part; the aliases unify the two so cross-topology extraction normalizes to one key. Grounded in real parts across manufacturers (TI LM4040 shunt, TI REF3025 series, ADI ADR4525 precision series). Condition axes for this family include V_IN, I_OUT, I_R (reverse/cathode current, shunt only), F, T_A, and the noise bandwidth axes BW_LOW/BW_HIGH. Aliases normalize the printed datasheet symbols; base-SI units keep cross-part comparison sound (ppm/degC for tempco, V for noise, ohm for dynamic impedance).

parameters.name: Reference output voltage
parameters.hint: The regulated reference voltage. For a series reference this is the output voltage; for a shunt reference it is the reverse breakdown voltage across the 2-terminal device (same concept, hence the shared aliases). Store the nominal with min/typ/max from the initial-accuracy spec at T_A = 25 degC.

parameters.name: Initial accuracy
parameters.hint: Output-voltage tolerance at 25 degC as a percentage. Vendors also print it as +/- absolute millivolts; capture the absolute case as min/typ/max under output_voltage and keep the percentage here. Grade suffixes (A/B/C/D) set this number.

parameters.name: Output voltage temperature coefficient
parameters.hint: The headline reference spec. Give the temperature range as a T_A condition (min..max). Note whether the datasheet uses the box (min/max) method or a typical average, in conditionsVerbatim. Grade suffixes set this number.

parameters.name: Quiescent / supply current
parameters.hint: Series references only: the current the reference draws to operate. Not applicable to a 2-terminal shunt (use minimum_operating_current there). Base-SI amps: a 42 uA IQ is 4.2e-05.

parameters.name: Minimum operating (cathode) current
parameters.hint: Shunt references only: the minimum reverse (cathode) current needed to reach and hold regulation. Below it the device is not in regulation.

parameters.name: Maximum operating (cathode) current
parameters.hint: Shunt references only: the maximum reverse current over which the reference is specified (distinct from the absolute-maximum reverse current, which is a stress limit).

parameters.name: Output current
parameters.hint: Series references only: the specified output source/sink current capability. Use min/max to bound a source(+)/sink(-) range.

parameters.name: Line regulation
parameters.hint: The fractional output-voltage change per volt of input change, ppm/V, the sibling of ppm/degC. Capture the printed ppm/V directly; a part printed in uV/V converts by dividing by VOUT (120 uV/V / 2.5 V = 48 ppm/V), or MAY use unit V/V instead when that is how it is specified. Give the V_IN range as a condition and keep the printed rate in conditionsVerbatim.

parameters.name: Load regulation
parameters.hint: The fractional output-voltage change per amp of load change, ppm/A. Datasheets print ppm/mA (multiply by 1000 for ppm/A) or a total ppm over a stated load range (divide by the range in A). A part printed in uV/mA MAY instead use the derived unit ohm (dVOUT/dIOUT). Source and sink can differ, so use one measurement per direction and keep the printed rate in conditionsVerbatim.

parameters.name: Dropout voltage / headroom
parameters.hint: Series references only: the minimum input-to-output differential (VIN - VOUT) for the output to stay in regulation.

parameters.name: Output voltage noise (peak-to-peak)
parameters.hint: Broadband output noise over a stated bandwidth, usually 0.1 Hz to 10 Hz peak-to-peak. Give BW_LOW and BW_HIGH as conditions. Convert uVpp to volts: 8 uVpp is 8e-06.

parameters.name: Output voltage noise density
parameters.hint: Spectral noise density at a stated frequency (give F as a condition). Convert nV/sqrtHz to V/sqrtHz: 150 nV/sqrtHz is 1.5e-07.

parameters.name: Dynamic output impedance
parameters.hint: Shunt references: the reverse dynamic impedance ZR (dVR/dIR), specified at an operating current and frequency. Series references: the small-signal output impedance. Give the operating current (I_R or I_OUT) and F as conditions; one measurement per point.

parameters.name: Power-supply rejection ratio
parameters.hint: Series references: rejection of input ripple, specified per frequency; one measurement per frequency point.

parameters.name: Long-term stability / drift
parameters.hint: Fractional output-voltage change over an elapsed time (commonly 1000 hours), in ppm as printed. Note the duration (and any temperature) in conditionsVerbatim.

parameters.name: Thermal hysteresis
parameters.hint: The residual fractional output-voltage shift after a temperature cycle back to 25 degC, in ppm (a datasheet printing 0.08% is 800 ppm). Sign is meaningful. Note the temperature cycle in conditionsVerbatim.

parameters.name: Turn-on settling time
parameters.hint: Time from power-up to the output settling within a stated error band. Note the error band and load in conditionsVerbatim.

parameters.name: Output capacitance range
parameters.hint: The output (load) capacitance range over which the reference is stable, as min..max in farads. Some references are stable with no output cap and specify only a maximum; others require a minimum. Note the ESR or dielectric in conditionsVerbatim if the datasheet constrains it.

parameters.name: Input capacitance
parameters.hint: Series references only: the recommended input/bypass capacitance. Store the recommended value or min in farads.

parameters.name: Input voltage range
parameters.hint: Series references only: the operating input-voltage range (limitClass recommended) and/or the absolute-maximum input voltage (limitClass absolute_max), distinguished by limitClass.

parameters.name: Operating temperature range
parameters.hint: The temperature range over which the device is specified. Store min and max.

parameters.name: Storage temperature range
parameters.hint: Absolute-maximum storage temperature range. Store as absolute_max with min and max.

parameters.name: Junction-to-ambient thermal resistance
parameters.hint: Thermal resistance from junction to ambient for the given package and board. Note the package/board in a condition.

parameters.name: Power dissipation
parameters.hint: Absolute-maximum power dissipation, temperature/derating dependent. Note T_A in a condition.

parameters.name: ESD, human body model
parameters.hint: ESD withstand, human body model. Store as absolute_max.

parameters.name: ESD, charged device model
parameters.hint: ESD withstand, charged device model. Store as absolute_max.
